title: thymus serpyllum var. citriodorus
intro: Thymus serpyllum var. citriodorus, commonly known as lemon thyme, is a fragrant, low-growing perennial herb belonging to the mint family. The medicinal properties of lemon thyme include its antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory, and antiseptic properties, which are attributed to its essential oils, particularly thymol and carvacrol. The health benefits of lemon thyme include its ability to boost the immune system, improve respiratory issues such as bronchitis and coughs, and its potential to reduce stress and anxiety. Lemon thyme can be used in various herbal preparations, including teas, infusions, and essential oil extracts, which can be used topically or in aromatherapy to promote relaxation and overall well-being.
